title: Sleight of Hand
wikitext:
[[File:143128_(2).gif|center|600x600px]]

== Summary ==
'''Sleight of Hand''' is an umbrella term for a variety of deceptive and dexterous movements. These movements are commonly associated with acts of misdirection, clever maneuvering, and they are frequently observed in fields ranging from stage magic, pickpocketing, tactical operations, and combat scenarios. While many individuals may possess a moderate level of movements, others demonstrate extraordinary proficiency—capable of performing feats that seem implausible to the casual observer.

These can include removing items from individuals in a crowd without being noticed, planting objects or weapons on multiple targets undetected, or executing complex manipulations with high precision under intense situations. It also can involve the coordination of various body parts, as well as the use of external tools such as knives, wires, coins, or pens. What distinguishes these actions is not merely their speed, but the level of skill, timing, and spatial awareness required to perform them effectively and without drawing attention.

== Possible Types ==

=== Sleight of Hand ===
A great example is Magicians; during their performance, they may show a card or coin, and within the blink of an eye make it disappear only to reappear behind a person's ear or in a pocket. Others are good at placing objects in spots they'd normally be hard to put for anyone untrained or moving objects around opponents attempting to grab them. Characters who can perform movements in front of a person but the person misses them, allowing them to get hand signals, hand signs, or hits in. Another is characters who use wires or threads to entrap someone or stop projectiles up close–but unnoticed, demonstrating complex hand movements in quick succession.

* '''Examples:''' '''[[Lupin III (Character)|Lupin III]]''', '''[[The Doctor (Doctor Who)|The Doctor]]''', '''[[Senjumaru Shutara]]'''

=== Pick-Pocketers ===
Pick-Pocketers are commonly shown to be people who, while walking around can unknowingly to others, take things out of their pockets or bags and swiftly hide them. More skilled thieves often times can steal an opponent's worn equipment, weapons, and any other important items they may utilize in a fight in order to both hinder them and offer new options to win. Conversely, a thief could reverse-pickpocket items onto a victim in order to deter them in some ways. This could result in point-blank explosions from a hidden bomb, having poisoned or cursed items hidden to sabotage inventories, having tracking devices placed, among many other possibilities depending on equipment and/or specific weaknesses.

* '''Examples:''' '''[[Catwoman (Post-Crisis)|Catwoman]]''', '''[[Gintoki Sakata]]''', '''[[Kaito Kuroba (Kaitou Kid)|Kaito Kuroba]]'''

=== Feints ===
A '''feint''' is an intentional deceptive maneuver intended to mislead an opponent during a fight. It involves initiating an action—such as a strike, movement, or posture—that convincingly suggests one intention, only to quickly shift to another, catching the opponent off-guard. The purpose is to provoke a premature or incorrect reaction, creating an opening for a real attack or escape.

* '''Examples:''' '''[[Mash Burnedead]]''', '''[[Chrollo Lucilfer]]''', '''[[Taro Sakamoto]]'''

== Limitations ==

* Opponents may be too far to reach for Pick-Pocketers.
*Pickpockets may find their tactics worthless against those who's items are bound to them or are capable of simply summoning it back at will.
* Some [[Enhanced Senses]] may make subtle movements easily seen such as the [[Sasuke Uchiha (Part II)|Sharingan]].
* Sleight of Hand may be unable to keep their actions going due to number of objects or opponents.

File:Nami_Pickpocket.jpeg|'''Pick-Pocketers:''' Nami robs her entire hometown without a single person noticing before she's long gone.
File:MHAPicker.jpeg|'''Pick-Pocketers:''' With Shoji's six arms, he's capable of pickpocketing people.
File:YfSfPOd.jpeg|'''Feints:''' Zekka can deliver blows not seen within his body's shadow.
File:Chroll.jpeg|'''Feints:''' Chrollo feints with a punch but swiftly changes his attack angle with a kick.
